A.evidence: Font-decoding failure: text dominated by corrupted symbol/dingbat glyphs where the page images show normal English; only a few markdown heading markers survive and all content is unreadable.

B.evidence: Prose mostly readable but Table 1 is entirely absent and part of the page-1/2 body text is dropped; literal 'GLYPH' tokens appear where special chars failed and 'Baden-Wurttember' is mangled.

C.evidence: Cleanest rendering: full Table 1 as a well-formed markdown table, correct Unicode (Wuerttemberg, degree signs, microfarads, diameter symbol, subscripts), complete heading hierarchy and faithful image placeholders.

D.evidence: Full table intact with correct Unicode, but systematic ligature loss drops the f in fi/ff/fl throughout (fth, gure, xes, specications, dierent, reective) and anchor-link cruft clutters the text.
